revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hakusan-jinja (Nuttari-higashi, Niigata)]][[ja:白山神社 (新潟市中央区沼垂東)]] {{Expand Japanese|白山神社 (新潟市中央区沼垂東)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hakusan Shrine (Q63148121) | native_name = 白山神社 | image = 沼垂白山神社拝殿.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hakusan Shrine (Q63148121) | coordinates = {{coord|37.91683333|139.07141667}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kikurihime|lt=Kikurihime|en|Kikurihime|ja|菊理媛神|mni|ꯀꯨꯀꯨꯔꯤꯍꯤꯃꯦ|zh|菊理媛命|qid=Q11619891|1=Kikurihime}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hakusan Shrine'''|白山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q125314093|ltq=Q125314093|qid=Q125314093|1=Hakusan shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11636502|ltq=Q11636502|qid=Q11636502|1=List of Shikinaisha in Echigo Province}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Echigo Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Echigo Province|ja|越後国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11636502|1=List of Shikinaisha in Echigo Province}} ** Series ordinal: 45 ** P155: {{ill|Q135040470|ltq=Q135040470|qid=Q135040470|1=Ishiwino Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Kahaahino Shrine|lt=Kahaahino Shrine|qid=Q135040472|1=Kahaahino Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q711787|ltq=Q711787|qid=Q711787|1=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} * {{ill|Q666514|ltq=Q666514|qid=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}} * {{ill|Nuttari District|lt=Nuttari District|ja|沼垂郡|qid=Q11554451|1=Nuttari District}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|37.91683333|139.07141667}} * {{coord|37.916643|139.071485}} == image (P18) == * 沼垂白山神社拝殿.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Kikurihime|lt=Kikurihime|en|Kikurihime|ja|菊理媛神|mni|ꯀꯨꯀꯨꯔꯤꯍꯤꯃꯦ|zh|菊理媛命|qid=Q11619891|1=Kikurihime}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182463 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|Q7311343|ltq=Q7311343|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * はくさんじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 950-0075https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7110005001372 == Commons category (P373) == * Hakusan-jinja (Nuttari-higashi, Niigata) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Mikuri Shrine|lt=Mikuri Shrine|qid=Q135040471|1=Mikuri Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '〒950-0075 新潟県新潟市中央区沼垂東１丁目１番１７号', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '新潟県新潟市中央区沼垂東', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hakusan-jinja (Nuttari-higashi, Niigata)}} [[JA:白山神社 (新潟市中央区沼垂東)]] {{wikidata link|Q63148121}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Hakusan Shrine in Nuttari-higashi, Chūō-ku, Niigata City|Niigata’s general tutelary Hakusan Shrine in Ichibanbori-dōri-chō, same ward|Hakusan Shrine (Ichibanbori-dōri-chō, Chūō-ku, Niigata City)}} {{神社 |名称 = Hakusan Shrine |画像 = [[File:沼垂白山神社拝殿.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niigata|Q179691|qid=Q179691|1=Niigata (city)}}{{ill|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata|Q711787|qid=Q711787|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Niigata}}{{ill|lt=Nuttari-higashi|Q81060683|qid=Q81060683|1=Nuttarihigashi, Chuo-ku}} 1-1-17 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|55|00.6|N|139|04|17.1|E|region:JP-15_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Nuttari Hakusan Shrine (ぬったりはくさんじんじゃ) |札所等 = |例祭 = August 18 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Hakusan Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hakusan Shrine''' (はくさんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} at 1-1-17 Nuttari-higashi, {{ill|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata|Q711787|qid=Q711787|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata|Q179691|qid=Q179691|1=Niigata (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. To distinguish it from the {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Ichibanbori-dōri-chō, Chūō-ku, Niigata City)|Q11579595|qid=Q11579595|lt=shrine of the same name|1=Hakusan Shrine}} in Ichibanbori-dōri-chō, Chūō Ward, Niigata City, it is called “Nuttari Hakusan Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === As of 2019, the enshrined deities are as follows: * '''{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}'''On-site information, dated April 13, 2019. * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' - deity of harmony, bonds, and living water. Also called Hakusan-hime. === History === The founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was once called '''Mikuri Shrine'''{{Cite book|ja|author=Niigata City Nuttari Library |title=Nuttari-ki |publisher=Niigata City Nuttari Library |year=1965 |page=6 |isbn=}}. It corresponds to '''Mikuri Shrine''' in Nuttari District, {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|qid=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}}, recorded in the '''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'''{{Cite book|ja|author=Shirai Eiji, Toki Masanori |title=Jinja Jiten (Shrine Dictionary) |publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan |year=1979 |page=273 |isbn=}}{{Cite book|ja|author=Nuttari Local History Study Group |title=Nuttari Monogatari |publisher=Niigata City Nuttari Library |year=1965 |page=2 |isbn=}}, but no source materials from the time of its founding remain. Nuttari lies in the area where the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|qid=Q734455|1=Shinano River}} and {{ill|lt=Agano River|Q390458|qid=Q390458|1=Agano River}} join and flow into the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|qid=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}}. “Mikuri” means water distribution, and is a deity of {{ill|lt=irrigation|Q11453|qid=Q11453|1=Irrigation}}. In the early Edo period (Kan'ei era), Nuttari Hakusan Shrine was in Ōse-yamaPresent-day Yamanoshita.{{Cite book|ja|author= |title=History of villages merged into Niigata City, Vol. 3 |publisher=Niigata City |year=1980 |page=168 |isbn=}} and served as the general tutelary shrine of the Nuttari area. Due to repeated flooding of the Shinano River, it was forced to relocate many times{{Cite book|ja|author= |title=History of villages merged into Niigata City, Vol. 3 |publisher=Niigata City |year=1980 |page=170 |isbn=}}. In May {{ill|lt=1706|Q6835|qid=Q6835|1=1706}} ({{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|qid=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 3), it moved to the western side of Rui-chi. Later, in {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|qid=Q6878|1=1714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 4), it moved further east, and in {{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|qid=Q6932|1=1725}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 10){{Cite book|ja|author=Niigata City History Editorial Committee (Ancient/Medieval section) |title=Niigata City History, General History 1 |publisher=Niigata City |year=1995 |page=198 |isbn=}} or {{ill|lt=1726|Q6936|qid=Q6936|1=1726}} (Kyōhō 11), it settled at its current location. Shrine treasures such as deity statues, ritual implements, and old books were lost through repeated flood damage and relocations. It was formerly a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. There are two Shikinaisha in Nuttari: Nuttari Hakusan Shrine and Goshiki Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kambara Shrine (Niigata)|Q63148107|qid=Q63148107|lt=Kambara Shrine|1=Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)}}). It is said that in the era of the {{ill|lt=Uesugi clan|Q1150058|qid=Q1150058|1=Uesugi clan}}, 100 koku of shrine land were donated, but records from {{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|qid=Q7706|1=1698}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 11) state “shrine land: 10 koku”{{Cite book|ja|author= |title=Kyōdo Niigata No.1 |publisher=Niigata Local History Research Society |year=1963 |page=198 |isbn=}}{{Cite book|ja|author=Nuttari 300-Year Settlement Festival Executive Committee |title=Nuttari |publisher=300-Year Festival Executive Committee |year=1984 |page=122 |isbn=}}. In the Edo period, it was an object of faith for the {{ill|lt=Mizoguchi clan|Q11564463|qid=Q11564463|1=Mizoguchi clan}} lords of {{ill|lt=Shibata Domain|Q1040856|qid=Q1040856|1=Shibata Domain}}, and Tokushō-in of the {{ill|lt=Shingon school|Q550182|qid=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}} in Nuttari served as its bettō temple{{Cite book|ja|author= |title=Kyōdo Niigata No.1 |publisher=Niigata Local History Research Society |year=1978 |page=25 |isbn=}}. === Precincts === In addition to the main sanctuary and worship hall, the precincts include subsidiary shrines, a shrine office, purification basin, dance hall, torii, a pair of komainu, and multiple stone lanterns. The oldest-dated stone lantern bears the inscription “Hakusan-gū”. On February 5, {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|qid=Q25245|1=2016}} (Heisei 28), a “temporary transfer festival” was held. The temporary transfer was done by the “{{ill|lt=hikiyā|Q826949|qid=Q826949|1=Structure relocation}}” method, moving the building without dismantling it. This was conducted in advance of full-scale work for widening Kurinoki Bypass, to move the sacred object to a temporary shrine. It was the first time in about 170 years that the sacred object left the main sanctuary{{Cite news |title=Prayers for safe movement of shrine building |newspaper=Niigata Nippo |date=2016-02-09 |author= |page=}}. On June 12 of the same year, the building (about 11 m tall and about 50 tons) was moved about 2 m toward the approach by hikiyā work again. It was carried out with cooperation from 500 people{{Cite news |title=Residents pull ropes to shift shrine building |newspaper=Niigata Nippo |date=2016-06-14 |author= |page=}}. * '''Main sanctuary and worship hall''' : The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|qid=Q7647|1=1848}} ({{ill|lt=Kōka|Q1325317|qid=Q1325317|1=Kōka}} 4). In that rebuilding, all timber used was {{ill|lt=hinoki cypress|Q917187|qid=Q917187|1=Chamaecyparis obtusa}}. The master carpenter was Kobayashi Gentarō of Kumagaya. Kobayashi Gentarō was a late-Edo master artisan called Hidari Jingorō, especially known for his carvings. * '''Principal image''' : Eleven-faced Kannon. Year of enshrinement is unknown{{Cite book|ja|author=Niigata Local History Research Society |title=Kyōdo Niigata No.1 |publisher=Niigata Local History Research Society |year=1963 |page=59 |isbn=}}. * '''Shrine office''' : Also called Sanshūden. * '''Waniguchi gong''' : Bears year inscription “Jōji 6, 丁未” (Shōhei 22, 1367 CE) and inscription of Haguro Gongen in Dewa Province. There is a record that it was originally seized and dedicated when the Uesugi attacked Dewa in the Tenshō era, but the timing differs by one year in sources and is uncertain{{Cite book|ja|author=Niigata Local History Research Society |title=Kyōdo Niigata No.1 |publisher=Niigata Local History Research Society |year=1963 |page=53 |isbn=}}. === Precinct shrines === [[File:沼垂白山神社 境内社.jpg|thumb|]] According to its plaque, the subsidiary precinct shrine enshrines Sugawara Ōkami, Komine Ōkami, Ōyama Ōkami, Akiba Ōkami, and Awashima Ōkami together. There are also materials listing the deities as Shinmei, Tenjin, and Inari. File:沼垂白山神社 鳥居.jpg File:沼垂白山神社 社務所.jpg File:沼垂白山神社 鳥居.jpg File:沼垂白山神社 遠景.jpg File:沼垂白山神社 手水.jpg File:沼垂白山神社 狛犬.jpg File:沼垂白山神社 灯籠.jpg === Outside-precinct shrine === [[File:沼垂白山神社 境外社.jpg|thumb|]] Sessha: '''Otogo Myōjin''' (おとごみょうじん) - Deity: === Festivals === Major festivals as of 2019 are as follows. * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1) * '''17th Night Festival''' (January 17) : A ritual lighting large candles and praying for happiness in the coming year. Parishioners and local residents visit to pray for family safety, business prosperity, and neighborhood peace{{Cite news |title=Yearly wishes entrusted to the light |newspaper=Niigata Nippo |date=2016-01-16 |author= |page=}}. * '''Tenjin Festival''' (February 25) * '''Komine Festival''' (March 1) * '''Inari Festival''' (early March) * '''Kinen Festival''' (mid-March) * '''Spring Festival''' (May 17–18) * '''Daisuke Festival''' (June 30) * '''Grand Festival''' (Nuttari Festival) (August 15–18) : Has over 200 years of tradition. By day, mikoshi processions parade through the town; at night, the main event “fighting lanterns” is held. Fighting lanterns involves colliding and pushing giant lanterns; held in part of the local shopping street, and is described as full of excitement from participants supporting the lanterns and spectators shouting “Hang in there!” “Push, push!”{{Cite news |title=Fighting lanterns clash with heat |newspaper=Niigata Nippo |date=2011-08-18 |author= |page=}}. * '''Shichi-go-san pilgrimage''' (November 15) * '''Autumn Festival''' (mid-November) * '''Daisuke Festival''' (November 31{{要検証||date=January 2024}}) * '''Monthly Festival''' (18th of every month) === Nuttari Festival === There are various theories on the origin of the Hakusan Festival, predecessor of the '''Nuttari Festival'''; its beginning dates to when the shrine was located at Ōse[https://web.archive.org/web/20181105112725/http://www.geocities.jp/nutsutarifest/yurairekisi.htm] Nuttari Festival homepage. The schedule is basically both days corresponding to old-calendar Tanabata: August 15 (eve festival) and 16 (main festival). In the eve festival, from 19:00, over about 30 minutes, a rite transferring the divine spirit to the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|qid=Q979940|1=Mikoshi}} is held inside Nuttari Hakusan Shrine. In the daytime part of the main festival, the mikoshi starts from Nuttari Hakusan Shrine at 8:00 AM. In the night part, mikoshi and lanterns from each neighborhood association gather at Inari Shrine and begin from there[https://web.archive.org/web/20181105112734/http://www.geocities.jp/nutsutarifest/gyoujiannnai.htm] Nuttari Festival homepage . Since 2014, students of Higashi Niigata Junior High School have been painting lantern pictures“Love for the region put into dragons,” Niigata Nippo, Aug. 15, 2018. The Nuttari Festival is held in the urban area of former {{ill|lt=Nuttari Town|Q11554450|qid=Q11554450|1=Nuttari}}Dissolved by merger in Taishō 3.[https://web.archive.org/web/20181105112734/http://www.geocities.jp/nutsutarifest/gyoujiannnai.htm] Nuttari Festival homepage . === Local information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niigata|Q179691|qid=Q179691|1=Niigata (city)}}{{ill|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata|Q711787|qid=Q711787|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} Nuttari-higashi 1-1-17 '''Access''' * About 15-minute walk east from {{ill|lt=Niigata Station|Q701753|qid=Q701753|1=Niigata Station}} * {{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū|Q11502827|qid=Q11502827|1=Niigata Kotsu}} routes '''{{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū bus route list#S9|Q11502828|qid=Q11502828|lt=S9|1=S9}}''' Kameda/Yokogoshi line, '''{{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū bus route list#E5|Q11502828|qid=Q11502828|lt=E5|1=E5}}''' Botan-yama line, '''{{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū bus route list#E6|Q11502828|qid=Q11502828|lt=E6|1=E6}}''' Takeo line, etc.; get off at “Nuttari Hakusan” stop{{Cite web|ja|url=https://transfer.navitime.biz/niigatabrt-newsystem/pc/diagram/BusAboardMap?stCode=00060470|title=“Nuttari Hakusan” boarding map |publisher = Navitime|accessdate =2020-03-30}}, about 2-minute walk * By car ** About 9 km from {{ill|lt=Niigata-Nishi Interchange|Q11083317|qid=Q11083317|lt=Niigata-Nishi IC|1=Niigata-nishi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Hokuriku Expressway|Q1549512|qid=Q1549512|1=Hokuriku Expressway}}, via {{ill|lt=National Route 116|Q6156734|qid=Q6156734|1=Japan National Route 116}} and {{ill|lt=National Route 8|Q640239|qid=Q640239|1=Japan National Route 8}}On-site information, April 13, 2019 '''Nearby''' * {{ill|lt=Nuttari Terrace|Q22123454|qid=Q22123454|1=Nuttari Terrace}} * {{ill|lt=Kambara Shrine (Niigata)|Q63148107|qid=Q63148107|lt=Kambara Shrine|1=Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)}} * {{ill|lt=Imayo Tsukasa Sake Brewery|Q55528819|qid=Q55528819|1=Imayo Tsukasa Sake Brewery}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="注"}} '''References''' {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Shirai Eiji, Toki Masanori, 'Jinja Jiten (Shrine Dictionary),' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1979 * Niigata City Nuttari Library, 'Nuttari-ki,' Niigata City Nuttari Library, 1965 * Niigata City Nuttari Library, 'Nuttari Monogatari,' Nuttari Local History Study Group, 1967 * Niigata City History Editorial Committee (Early Modern section), 'Niigata City History, General History 1: Ancient/Medieval/Early Modern (Part 1),' Niigata City, 1995 * Niigata City History Editorial Committee (Early Modern section), 'Niigata City History, General History 2: Early Modern (Part 2),' Niigata City, 1997 * 'Nuttari,' Nuttari 300-Year Settlement Festival Executive Committee, 1984 * 'Kyōdo Niigata,' No.1, Niigata Local History Research Society, 1963 * 'History of villages merged into Niigata City,' Vol. 3, Niigata City, 1980 * Niigata Prefecture General Affairs Department, Documents and Education Affairs Division, 'Niigata Prefecture Religious Corporation Directory (as of Mar. 31, 1976),' Niigata Prefecture, 1976 Also refers to on-site signboards (as of Apr. 20, 2019), and Niigata Nippo issues of Aug. 18, 2011; Jan. 19, 2016; Feb. 9, 2016; Jun. 14, 2016; Jan. 19, 2017; Mar. 31, 2018; and Aug. 15, 2018. === External links === * [https://shinmeigu.jpwaza.com/%e6%b2%bc%e5%9e%82%e7%99%bd%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-2/ Nuttari Hakusan Shrine] - Official shrine site {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hakusan Shrine (Q63148121)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hakusan Shrine (Q63148121)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]]
